result.summary: Mapped Ably’s public developer integration surface: Control API, REST API, SSE API, and Ably CLI; confirmed access-token auth for Control API/CLI and API-key auth paths for SSE, while raw REST API auth mechanics were not fully pinned from the scraped docs.

ably_control_access_token.label: Ably access token
ably_control_access_token.setup: In the Ably dashboard, open [Access tokens](https://ably.com/users/access_tokens). Click **Create new token**, choose the account, expiry, and capabilities, then copy the token value immediately — Ably shows the full token only once. These access tokens authenticate the [Control API](https://ably.com/docs/platform/account/control-api) and the [Ably CLI](https://ably.com/docs/platform/tools/cli).

ably_api_key.label: Ably API key
ably_api_key.setup: Create or manage API keys for an app from the Ably dashboard or via the [Control API key endpoints](https://ably.com/docs/api/control-api) / [CLI auth key commands](https://ably.com/docs/platform/account/access-tokens). Ably documents API keys as the credential used for [Basic auth](https://ably.com/docs/auth/basic); the key is used as the username-like secret pair value Ably SDKs and raw HTTP clients send to Ably. Treat it as server-side only and never expose it to untrusted clients.
